D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Tyrian Purple — 12,000 Murex Snails per Gram

Phoenician dyers extracted 6,6'-dibromoindigo from the hypobranchial gland of Murex sea snails; ~12,000 snails per gram of dye made imperial purple worth its weight in gold

The chromophore is 6,6'-dibromoindigo — chemically related to indigo but with two bromines from the marine environment. Each Murex brandaris contributes a tiny drop of yellow precursor; sun exposure converts it to purple. The shell middens at Tyre (Lebanon) reach 40 m high — millennia of accumulated waste.

Drag snail count; the cumulative dye yield increases linearly.
